mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points over one month signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending confidence and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or unemployment rate declines without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) create stagflationary pressures that erode corporate margins and investor confidence, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of instances where both CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS remains below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) during late-cycle expansion phases indicate persistent demand pressures, which correlate with elevated equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of periods with HOUST growth above 1.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by > 1.0% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Excessive job growth (PAYEMS) above 100k per month indicates labor market overheating, prompting monetary tightening expectations that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances with PAYEMS growth above 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Positive interactions between rising consumer spending (RSAFS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicate strong economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases > 1.0% and UNRATE declines > 0.2pp monthly
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of instances where RSAFS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) during early-cycle phases indicates expanding economic activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 30% of periods with GDPC1 growth above 2.0% quarterly.
